title: dysoxylum pachyrhache
intro: Dysoxylum pachyrhachis is a species of plant in the family Meliaceae, commonly known as the 'Indian white wood' or 'white wood'. It is a deciduous tree native to Southeast Asia, known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine. The bark and roots of the plant are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. In herbal preparations, the bark and roots are often made into decoctions, infusions, or tinctures, and can be used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions.
